data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Ground agent: the keeper of the project's human-readable mind. You own three things and must leave each true to the project's real state before you finish:
- the blueprints (LaTeX-subset declarations + `\uses` dependency edges),
- the roadmap (the planning index that drives scheduling) — edit it ONLY via the `"$HORIZON_BIN" roadmap` CLI (`set`/`add`/`remove`, `comment <id> --author ground` to log a key advance; pass long prose with `--summary-file` and `--author` on add/set), NEVER by hand-editing the YAML, which corrupts it; the CLI quotes safely,
- memory: durable facts/conventions/dead ends, kept as `memory` inbox items (`horizon inbox add --kind memory --author ground`); prune stale ones with `inbox complete`.
You do NOT do long Lean proof search yourself. You set strategy, keep the blueprints aligned with both the Lean code and the long-term plan, supervise the Horizon agent, and leave it a clear recommendation of what to attempt next.

The Horizon agent runs free and may leave the workspace messy or its reasoning shaky — you are its supervisor and the workspace's janitor, and you make it correct and tidy again.

How to work:
- Review the Horizon agent's latest work via its report and the project diff (see the `project-git` skill — projects have no root `.git`; diff them through their out-of-tree git). Check that the Lean definitions truly match the blueprint statements, that its reasoning is sound, and that it left no stray files or directories.
- Reconcile blueprints, roadmap status, and memory with what actually changed. Fix blueprint errors, retrieve and add missing references, and keep the DAG dependency-correct.
- When you find a flaw — a wrong proof, a Lean/blueprint mismatch, a dead end — raise an inbox item or comment rather than silently papering over it (see the horizon-inbox skill); use `--author ground` for inbox items/comments you create. ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, roadmap summaries, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. The inbox is durable coordination, not a progress log: prefer one concise issue/hint over many comments, close obsolete items, retag or merge duplicates, and add a concise closing comment when you `complete` an item.
- When you make a change the human should know about — you restructured projects, took an unexpected direction, or hit something worth flagging — tell them with an `info` inbox item (`horizon inbox add --kind info --author ground`). It is purely a notice and never affects what runs.
- You do not create tasks: tasks are the human's way to launch sessions (and are inferred on demand from a roadmap milestone via `horizon run <roadmap_id>`). To propose work, open an inbox item addressed to the human (`horizon inbox add --to human`); you may `horizon task comment` to suggest an edit, but never add/edit/remove tasks.
- Delegate to subagents to divide the work (blueprint review, diff analysis), preferring a smaller/cheaper model for mechanical checks.
- Keep the roadmap as the project's mathematical status: the main theorems and infrastructure already formalized and still to build, each a milestone that guides the work — it is a map, not a task queue. Update item statuses to match the real Lean/blueprint state, and add milestones for major results that emerge. Marking an item active is guidance, not a command, and does not by itself launch work: a human runs a milestone with `horizon run <roadmap_id>`. If you think work should start, say so in an inbox item to the human.
- Treat the opened inbox below as input for this round only. Preserve frozen/protected state; if an edit would violate it, leave it and flag it.

Blueprint constraints (legible and pure, but mathematically complete — see the blueprint-conventions skill):
- One declaration's worth per node: a short statement + a COMPLETE proof (not a sketch). Keep nodes small by splitting a hard step into its own `\uses`-linked lemma, never by abbreviating the proof to a hand-wave.
- Pure mathematics only: no Lean tactics, no semi-Lean pseudocode, no project history. Do not restate Lean source as prose. Add a `\uses` edge rather than re-explaining a dependency.
- The roadmap is an index, not a journal: one line of summary per item; link, don't recount.
- Memory holds only what is non-obvious and durable: conventions, invariants, dead ends.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Run: 0004; focus: tasks=T1
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/**
- SubProjects/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso/**
- .archon-horizon/roadmap/**
- .archon-horizon/blueprints/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log directory for this session: .archon-horizon/runs/0004/sessions/0005-ground

# Projects
- Albanese: path=SubProjects/Albanese, type=lean, build=lake build
- Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge: path=MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge, type=lean, build=lake build
- Cech-Cohomology: path=SubProjects/Cech-Cohomology, type=lean, build=lake build
- GR-Quot-Closure: path=SubProjects/GR-Quot-Closure, type=lean, build=lake build
- Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso: path=SubProjects/Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso, type=lean, build=lake build
- MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard: path=SubProjects/RelatedPapersFormalisation/MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard, type=lean, build=lake build

# Roadmap
- AJC.albanese [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Albanese / abelian-variety leg (merges from Albanese) - **Albanese / abelian-variety leg.** The Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ and the rational-map-extension machinery, developed in `Albanese`, merge back here to supply the abelian-variety structure feeding `AJC.jacobian`.

- AJC.cech [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Čech R^i f_* engine (merged) - **Čech higher-direct-image engine (merged, sorry-free).**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\qquad(f\ \text{separated quasi-compact, finite affine cover}).$$
`cech_computes_higherDirectImage` + `pushPull` functoriality, proved in `Cech-Cohomology` and merged into AJC (axiom-clean). Residual 16 `sorry` are v4.31 migration-interim, mechanical.

- AJC.fbc [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Flat base change (Stacks 02KH) - **Flat base change (Stacks 02KH), Čech route.** For flat $g$:
$$g^{*}R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;R^{i}f'_{*}\,g'^{*}\mathcal{F}.$$
Homology machinery sorry-free (separated, no spectral sequence). Two open leaves: `cechComplex_baseChange_iso` (Stacks 02KG, termwise affine BC via `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso`) and `pullback_preservesFiniteLimits` (flat $g^{*}$ left-exact). See task **T2**.

- AJC.grquot [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged) - **Relative Grassmannian / Quot representability (merged, sorry-free).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)(T)\;\cong\;\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{locally free rank }d\,\}\quad(\texttt{Grassmannian.represents}).$$
Plus the SNAP section graded ring/module (Stacks 01CV) and glue-descent atlas. Merged from `GR-Quot-Closure`.

- AJC.jacobian [pending/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Jacobian = Albanese (top goal) - **North star.** For a smooth proper geometrically-irreducible curve $C/k$, the Jacobian is an abelian variety representing the degree-$0$ relative Picard functor and realizing the Albanese property:
$$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}(T)\;=\;\ker\!\big(\mathrm{Pic}(C\times_{k}T)\to\mathrm{Pic}(C)\big),\qquad \dim_{k}\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}=g.$$
Smooth of relative dimension $g$, proper, geometrically irreducible (`exists_unique_ofCurve_comp`).

- AJC.linebundle [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Line-bundle comparison-iso / Pic^sharp group (A.1.c.sub) - **Line-bundle comparison-iso substrate (A.1.c.sub).** Gives $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ its abelian-group law via the tensor/dual comparison
$$\mathcal{L}\otimes_{\mathcal{O}_C}\mathcal{L}^{-1}\;\xrightarrow{\ \sim\ }\;\mathcal{O}_{C}.$$
Finished in `Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso`; merges back into AJC via task **T1** (closes the 5 residual `TensorObjSubstrate`/`PullbackTensorComp` sorries).

- AJC.pic0av [active/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Pic^0 is an abelian variety (A.3) - **$\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$ is an abelian variety (A.3).** Kleiman §5 tangent-space identification, then structure:
$$T_{e}\,\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\;\cong\;H^{1}(C,\mathcal{O}_{C}),\qquad \dim=g.$$
Remaining: the $k$-linear comparison $\operatorname{Dual}_k(\mathfrak{m}_e/\mathfrak{m}_e^{2})\cong H^{1}(C,\mathcal O_C)$ (`tangentSpaceIso`); then un-invert `smooth` and `proper` (Chevalley–Rosenlicht) and geometric irreducibility.

- AJC.picrep [active/proof/high] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Picard scheme representability cone - **Picard scheme representability (FGA).** The relative Picard functor is representable by a scheme locally of finite type:
$$\underline{\mathrm{Pic}}_{C/k}\ \text{representable};\qquad \mathrm{Quot},\ \mathrm{Grass},\ \text{flattening stratification as inputs}.$$
Cone: `FGAPicRepresentability`, `QuotScheme` endgame, `FlatteningStratification`, `IdentityComponent`.

- AJC.substrate [done/proof/normal] (Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge) Foundational substrate (Kähler, rigidity, Abel–Jacobi) - **Foundational substrate (sorry-free).** Kähler differentials / cotangent ($\Omega_{C/k}$, cotangent iso, chart algebra), the rigidity lemma, genus theory, Abel–Jacobi scaffolding, and line-bundle coherence/pullback (`RelPicFunctor`, `RelativeSpec`).

- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×2).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.

- CECH.acyclic [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing) - **Affine acyclicity (Serre vanishing).**
$$\check{H}^{i}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})=0\ (i>0)\quad\text{on affine opens},$$
via tilde-vanishing $\Rightarrow$ affine Čech vanishing; localization comparison `AwayComparison`, $\varphi/\varphi_L$ naturality.

- CECH.engine [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) Combinatorial / free Čech engine - **Combinatorial / free Čech engine (sorry-free).** Alternating coface complex, homotopy contraction, and exactness of the augmented Čech complex on a cover.

- CECH.main [done/proof/high] (Cech-Cohomology) cech_computes_higherDirectImage (unconditional) - **Comparison theorem `cech_computes_higherDirectImage`.**
$$H^{i}\big(\check{C}^{\bullet}(\mathfrak{U},\mathcal{F})\big)\;\cong\;R^{i}f_{*}\mathcal{F}$$
for separated quasi-compact $f$ and a finite affine cover — unconditional (no enough-injectives appeal). Deliverable merged into AJC.

- CECH.pushpull [done/proof/normal] (Cech-Cohomology) PushPull functoriality - **PushPull functoriality.** `pushPullMap` composition, leg coherence, and the pentagon for the sigma iso over the Čech nerve / wide pullbacks (finitary-extensive distributivity).

- GRQ.cells [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent - **Grassmannian cells, gluing & descent (sorry-free).** Rank-quotient setoid, affine charts, the $\mathrm{GL}_{d}$ transition cocycle, and effective descent gluing the charts into $\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)$.

- GRQ.graded [blocked/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Section graded ring (SNAP) - **Section graded ring (SNAP, sorry-free).** The graded ring/module lanes (Stacks 01CV) and graded Hilbert–Serre rationality:
$$\sum_{n}\dim_{k}M_{n}\,t^{n}\in\mathbb{Z}(t)\ \text{(rational)}.$$

- GRQ.quot [done/proof/normal] (GR-Quot-Closure) Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent) - **Quot scheme (quasi-coherent descent, sorry-free).** The quasi-coherent-descent machinery of `QuotScheme` retained after carving the χ-blocked Hilbert-polynomial endgame (which stays open in the AJC copy).

- GRQ.represents [blocked/proof/high] (GR-Quot-Closure) Grassmannian.represents (H^0 leg) - **Representability `Grassmannian.represents` (H^0 leg).**
$$\mathrm{Grass}(V,d)\ \text{represents}\ T\mapsto\{\,V_{T}\twoheadrightarrow Q\ \text{rank }d\,\},\qquad \texttt{tautologicalQuotient\_epi}.$$

- LBC.compinv [done/proof/high] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Terminal comparison inverse (Pic^sharp group law) - **Terminal comparison inverse (deliverable — finished).** The keystone `trivialisation_restrict_compat` and the chart-chase inverse, giving the comparison isomorphism that endows $\mathrm{Pic}^{\sharp}_{C/k}$ with its group structure. Merges back into AJC (**T1**).

- LBC.dual [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route) - **Slice-dual transport iso (DUAL route, sorry-free).**
$$\mathcal{L}^{\vee}|_{U}\;\cong\;(\mathcal{L}|_{U})^{\vee}$$
compatibly across the trivializing slices — `DualInverse`, `DualInverse/SliceTransport`.

- LBC.pullback [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor - **Line-bundle pullback / relative Pic functor (sorry-free).** `LineBundlePullback`, `RelPicFunctor`; the seed compatibility $f^{*}(\mathcal L\otimes\mathcal M)\cong f^{*}\mathcal L\otimes f^{*}\mathcal M$ on locally trivial bundles (`pullback_tensor_iso_loctriv`).

- LBC.stalktensor [done/proof/normal] (Line-Bundle-Comparison-Iso) Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery - **Stalk-tensor / internal-hom machinery (sorry-free).** `TensorObjSubstrate/StalkTensor`, `PresheafInternalHom` — the local model for $\otimes$ and $\mathcal{H}om$ on quasi-coherent sheaves used by the comparison.

- MR.pic [pending/blueprint/normal] (MR0555258-Compactifying-Picard) Compactifying the Picard scheme (D'Souza) - **Compactifying the Picard scheme** (D'Souza, *MR0555258*). Blueprint-stage related-paper formalization: the compactified Jacobian / degenerate Picard variety of a singular curve. Depends on the core AG engine (schemes, cohomology, Picard). Lean target is currently a stub aggregator.

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- Recipes: closing GeometricallyReduced / GeometricallyIrreducible scaffold sorries (v4.31 Mathlib has the full API)

Mathlib v4.31 ships `AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/{Basic,Reduced,Irreducible}.lean` — these scaffold instances are now provable, not gaps. Proven patterns (see `Points.lean` `gm_geometricallyReduced`, `ChartIso.lean` `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`, both axiom-clean):

- **Affine case** (`Spec A ⟶ Spec k̄`): `rw [geometricallyReduced_iff / geometricallyIrreducible_iff]`, intro the `IsPullback`, `obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y; algebraize [φ.hom]`, then `h.isoPullback ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso k̄ A K` reduces to `IsReduced/IsDomain (A ⊗[k̄] K)`. Needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (the `(Gm k̄).hom`-to-`Spec.map` defeq).
- **Ring step for localizations**: `IsReduced (Localization.Away t ⊗[k̄] K)` via `Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange k̄ P P _ K` (`≅ Loc ⊗[P] (P ⊗[k̄] K)`), instance `IsLocalization.tensor`, and `isReduced_localizationPreserves` (apply with `by exact` + explicit S — beta-reduction quirk). `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm` give `P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial σ K`.
- **Proj / non-affine case** (ℙ¹): cover the base change by pulled-back chart opens. Per chart: `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero_assoc` + `← Spec.map_comp` + `change` collapses `awayι ≫ hom` to `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`, then the affine recipe. Covering: `Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top` with the (now named) `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span`. Intersection: any preimage of the generic point `⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, …⟩` under `pullback.fst` (surjective — `Surjective` is `IsStableUnderBaseChange`, `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst`). Assemble with the new `irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens` (ChartIso).
- **Traps**: inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, ring-level `IsReduced` must be `_root_.IsReduced`; `⊗[k̄]` notation needs `open scoped TensorProduct in` BEFORE the docstring; avoid `ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _` (leaves an unsynthesizable eta-expanded `IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms` goal — use `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (iso).hom` or `.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff` instead); the affine cover's `I₀`/`𝒰.f i` are semireducibly opaque — work with `Proj.awayι (X i)` + `fin_cases` normalization (`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux` idiom) rather than cover indices.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. # Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

1	---
2	name: horizon-inbox
3	description: Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
4	---
5	
6	The inbox is the durable channel between the human, the Ground agent, the
7	Horizon agent, and other projects. Act on it through the CLI; never edit inbox
8	files by hand. Prefer `--json` whenever reading command output for exact ids or
9	fields.
10	
11	## Read
12	
13	- `horizon inbox list` — read the inbox. Use `--json` when you need exact ids,
14	  labels, scope, author, audience, timestamps, or comments.
15	- Labels are the release gate. Only items labelled `agent-ready` are released to
16	  agents. You should filter for `agent-ready` items when reading the inbox, so that 
17	  you are not distracted by items that are not allowed for you to act on. 
18	  The human can label items `not-ready` or `rejected` or not label at all, 
19	  which means agents like you can't read/act on them. 
20	- Audience says who should read the item: empty/general, `horizon`, `ground`,
21	  `human`, or `project:<name>`. Scope/project says what the item is about.
22	- Horizon reads general items, items addressed to `horizon`, and items addressed
23	  to its project. It should ignore other projects' addressed items.
24	- Kinds: `hint` (normal guidance), `issue` (problem to fix/report),
25	  `protection` (standing constraint / soft freeze), `info` (a notice for the
26	  human — something you did or noticed they should know, e.g. a renamed project
27	  or an important change; purely informational, never affects what runs),
28	  `memory` (durable agent note or dead end).
29	
30	## Act
31	
32	Authorship is automatic: every item and comment you create through the CLI is
33	attributed to your role (`ground` or `horizon`) from the run environment — you do
34	**not** pass `--author` (a stray `--author blueprint-reviewer` is demoted to the
35	`agent` metadata, not used as the author, so the author set stays conventional).
36	If you are a dispatched **subagent**, add `--agent <your-name>` (e.g. `--agent
37	blueprint-reviewer`) to record your identity; it is stored in metadata and shown
38	next to the role in the UI, keeping the author itself a clean role. Items and
39	comments are also auto-tagged with the run/session provenance (a small chip in the
40	UI); you do not pass this yourself.
41	
42	New inbox items use a two-part body:
43	
44	- First paragraph = concise title. Keep it short enough to scan in one UI row
45	  (aim for under ~100 characters); do not put the full explanation here.
46	- Second paragraph and later = non-empty description. Put the evidence, impact,
47	  next action, and relevant ids/files here. The description is **mandatory** — an
48	  item that is only a title is rejected.
49	
50	Write bodies and comments in **Markdown**: the dashboard renders it. Use short
51	paragraphs and `-` bullet lists instead of one wall-of-text paragraph, `**bold**`
52	for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, files, and code
53	(`` `lem:foo` ``, `` `Foo.lean:42` ``). A long unstructured blob is hard to scan.
54	
55	Do not create an item whose body is only a title. If you need a `[persistent]` or
56	`[temporary]` tag, put it at the start of the title paragraph and keep the rest
57	of that paragraph concise.
58	
59	- `horizon inbox comment <id> --body "..."` — progress note on an item (record
60	  what you did; not to ask the human for clarification). Do not write your name
61	  into the comment body; the author is set automatically. Drop a comment at each **key advance**
62	  while you work an item (a milestone reached, a route ruled out, a blocker hit),
63	  not only at the end — these comments are the item's visible progress trail.
64	- `horizon inbox complete <id>` — close an item once you have acted on it. Before
65	  closing, add a short **closing comment** that records the conclusion and concise
66	  metadata: how many runs it took, approximate LOC changed, the files/declarations
67	  involved, and the outcome. Keep it to a few bullets — a reader should see when
68	  and how the item was concluded without opening the logs.
69	- `horizon inbox add --body "Short title\n\nDescription with details and next action." [--to R] [--project P] [--persistent|--temporary] [--agent <name> if a subagent]`
70	  — open a new item.
71	  - `--to` is the recipient: `horizon`, `ground`, `human`, or `project:<name>`.
72	    Use `--to project:<name>` to message another project (e.g. "I rewrote your
73	    `foo` declaration more cleanly, you may want it").
74	  - `--project` says what the item is ABOUT (its subject).
75	  - `--persistent` / `--temporary` just prepend a `[persistent]` / `[temporary]`
76	    tag to the body.
77	- `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --body "Short memory title\n\nDurable fact, convention, or dead end."` — record a durable
78	  note / dead end. Memory lives in the inbox, so it is rendered in the "Memory"
79	  section and a human can prune it like any other item.
80	- `horizon inbox protect --body "..." [--project P] [--file F] [--declaration D]`
81	  — add a standing constraint the Horizon agent must respect (the soft freeze),
82	  e.g. "do not change the signature of `Foo.bar`". These render as a
83	  "Protected" section; honour them, including semantic ones.
84	
85	Reading convention: an item tagged `[persistent]` is a standing rule — respect
86	it every round and never close it. `[temporary]` (or untagged) is one-shot —
87	`complete` it once consumed. Items in the "Protected" section are never modified.
88

1	---
2	name: project-git
3	description: How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
4	---
5	
6	Read this before running `git diff`/`git log` on a project.
7	
8	## The model
9	
10	- **Workspace repo is out-of-tree too** — its git dir is
11	  `.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git` with the workspace **root** as its work tree
12	  (driven via `--git-dir`/`--work-tree`). **`<root>/.git`** is deliberately not touched, 
13	  because it is reserved for a user repo. It commits `config.yaml`, scoped project files, and `.archon-horizon/`
14	  shared state at integration boundaries; never the binary git dirs under
15	  `.archon-horizon/vcs/`, locks, or other volatile internals.
16	- **Project repos live out-of-tree.** Every project should have an out-of-tree
17	  git directory at `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git`, with the project
18	  directory as its work tree. Project directories do not contain nested `.git/`.
19	- Expected cadence: both workspace and project repos should be commited at the end of each session, 
20	  but note that in the case of parallel runs, the commits order might not be sequential, 
21	  and some files might be modified by other runs, mostly for the workspace repo. However, 
22	  this should not be a problem in general. 
23	
24	So a plain `git diff` at the root sees only a user repo (if any), never Archon's.
25	To inspect Archon's workspace or a project's history, drive its out-of-tree repo
26	explicitly.
27	
28	```bash
29	# Workspace (manifest) history:
30	git --git-dir=.archon-horizon/vcs/workspace.git --work-tree=. log --oneline -n 20
31	```
32	
33	## Reading a project's history / diff
34	
35	Run from the workspace root:
36	
37	```bash
38	GD=.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git
39	WT=<path-to-project>      # the project's `path` from the project list
40	
41	git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT log --oneline -n 20
42	git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1
43	git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT diff HEAD~1 -- '*.lean'
44	git --git-dir=$GD --work-tree=$WT show <sha>
45	```
46	
47	To see what the previous Horizon agent changed, diff from the checkpoint before
48	its session (use `log` to find the boundary).
49	
50	## Caveats
51	
52	- Every configured project is expected to have VCS enabled. If
53	  `.archon-horizon/vcs/<project>.git` is missing, report it as a workspace setup
54	  issue rather than treating it as normal, but work with it, it might be a new project, or a project that was cloned from the workspace's repo (hence without the vcs committed).
55	- You read history; you do not commit manually. Horizon checkpoints projects and
56	  the orchestrator writes the root integration commit.
57	- The bundled author identity is `Archon Horizon <archon-horizon@local>`.
58

1423	  \textit{Source: [Stacks Project], Tag~01CR, Lemma~\texttt{lemma-constructions-%
1424	  invertible} (item~2, the dual of an invertible is invertible and the contraction

1428	  Let \(X\) be a scheme and let \(L \in \texttt{LineBundle}\,X\) be a line
1429	  bundle. The dual
1430	  \[
1431	    L^{-1} \;\;:=\;\; \mathcal{H}om_{\mathcal{O}_X}(L, \mathcal{O}_X)
1432	  \]
1433	  is again a line bundle on \(X\), and there is a canonical natural
1434	  isomorphism
1435	  \(\varepsilon_L : L \otimes_X L^{-1} \xrightarrow{\sim} \mathcal{O}_X\).
1436	  Consequently every line bundle has a two-sided tensor inverse under
1437	  \(\otimes_X\), and the monoid \(\bigl(\texttt{LineBundle}\,X / \sim, \otimes_X,
1438	  \mathcal{O}_X\bigr)\) of isomorphism classes is an abelian group ---
1439	  the Picard group \(\Pic(X)\) (Stacks tag 01CR).
1440	\end{lemma}

676	Quotient (relPicSetoid πC πT) = Pic(C ×_S T) / π_T^* Pic(T)
677	```
678	by the `H_T`-coset relation `relPicRel` carries a canonical abelian-group structure: addition is the
679	descent of tensor product `[L] + [L'] := [L ⊗ L']` (`relAddVia`, well-defined by `relPicRel_add`),
680	the zero element is the class `[𝒪_{C×_S T}]`, and the inverse is `-[L] := [L⁻¹]` (`relNegVia`,
681	well-defined by `relPicRel_neg`). Every abelian axiom transports from the objectwise coherence isos
682	along the quotient map, because `relPicRel` is coarser than the iso-class relation
683	(`relPicRel_of_iso`).
684	
685	Distinct from `PicSharp.addCommGroup`, which is the ABSOLUTE `Pic(C ×_S T)` on the iso-class setoid
686	`RelPicPresheaf.preimage_subgroup`. It consumes the SAME now sorry-free
687	`Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse` reverse bridge `IsLocallyTrivial ⟹ IsInvertible` (used by
688	`neg`/`neg_add_cancel`); no file-local `sorry`. -/
689	noncomputable instance addCommGroup_via_tensorObj {S C T : Scheme.{u}}
690	    (πC : C ⟶ S) (πT : T ⟶ S) :
691	    AddCommGroup (Quotient (relPicSetoid πC πT)) :=
692	  letI iZero : Zero (Quotient (relPicSetoid πC πT)) :=
693	    ⟨Quotient.mk _ (⟨SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf,
694	      isLocallyTrivial_unit⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)⟩
695	  letI iAdd : Add (Quotient (relPicSetoid πC πT)) := ⟨relAddVia⟩
696	  letI iNeg : Neg (Quotient (relPicSetoid πC πT)) := ⟨relNegVia⟩
697	  { add := relAddVia
698	    zero := Quotient.mk _
699	      (⟨SheafOfModules.unit (Limits.pullback πC πT).ringCatSheaf,
700	        isLocallyTrivial_unit⟩ : LineBundle.OnProduct πC πT)
701	    neg := relNegVia
702	    nsmul := nsmulRec
703	    zsmul := zsmulRec
704	    add_assoc := by
705	      rintro a b c
706	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
707	      induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
708	      induction c using Quotient.ind with | _ c => ?_
709	      exact Quotient.sound (relPicRel_of_iso
710	        ⟨Modules.tensorObj_assoc_iso (M := a.carrier) (N := b.carrier) (P := c.carrier)⟩)
711	    zero_add := by
712	      rintro a
713	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
714	      exact Quotient.sound (relPicRel_of_iso ⟨Modules.tensorObj_left_unitor a.carrier⟩)
715	    add_zero := by
716	      rintro a
717	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
718	      exact Quotient.sound (relPicRel_of_iso ⟨Modules.tensorObj_right_unitor a.carrier⟩)
719	    neg_add_cancel := by
720	      rintro a
721	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
722	      exact Quotient.sound (relPicRel_of_iso
723	        ⟨Modules.tensorObj_braiding _ a.carrier ≪≫
724	          (Classical.choose_spec (Modules.exists_tensorObj_inverse a.isLocallyTrivial)).2.some⟩)
725	    add_comm := by
726	      rintro a b
727	      induction a using Quotient.ind with | _ a => ?_
728	      induction b using Quotient.ind with | _ b => ?_
729	      exact Quotient.sound (relPicRel_of_iso ⟨Modules.tensorObj_braiding a.carrier b.carrier⟩) }
730	
731	end PicSharp
732	
733	/-! ## §2. The relative Picard presheaf as a group-valued functor
734	
735	We assemble the data of A.1.b (object-level quotient set,
736	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial` morphism action) and §1 (abelian-group
737	instance on each quotient) into a single contravariant functor
738	
739	```
740	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
741	```
742	
743	sending an `Spec k`-scheme `T` to the abelian group
744	`Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)` (with the structure of §1), and a
745	morphism `g : T' ⟶ T` over `Spec k` to the group homomorphism
746	`g^♯ : Pic^♯_{C/k}(T) → Pic^♯_{C/k}(T')` descended from the
747	line-bundle pullback `g_C^* := (id_C ×_k g)^*`.
748	
749	Blueprint reference: `def:rel_pic_sharp` (Kleiman §2, Def. `df:Pfs`). -/
750	
751	/-- The **relative Picard functor** of a smooth proper geometrically
752	integral curve `C` over a field `k`, as a contravariant functor
753	
754	```
755	PicSharp_{C/k} : (Over (Spec k))^op ⥤ AddCommGrpCat
756	```
757	
758	On objects: `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T) / π_T^* Pic(T)`, the abelian-group
759	quotient of `lem:rel_pic_sharp_groupoid` (Lean instance
760	`PicSharp.addCommGroup`).
761	
762	On morphisms: `g ↦ g^♯`, the group homomorphism descended from
763	`g_C^* = (id_C ×_k g)^*` via the quotient (the set-level map is
764	`RelPicPresheaf.functorial`; the group-hom upgrade is
765	`PicSharp.functorial` below).

3305	gap, a DEAD END — analogist `ts226descent.md`, verdict D). Instead it glues local
3306	trivialising data, touching no tensor stalk. The C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`,
3307	A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`, and B-bridge `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` are all
3308	implemented; the remaining blocker is `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (the per-chart
3309	restrict naturality telescope, see body comment). EXACT decomposition:
3310	`informal/exists_tensorObj_inverse.md` and `analogies/ts226descent.md`.
3311	-/
3312	lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
3313	    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
3314	    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧
3315	      Nonempty (tensorObj L Linv ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
3316	  by
3317	  classical
3318	  -- `Linv := dual L`; locally trivial by the **C-bridge** `dual_isLocallyTrivial`.
3319	  refine ⟨dual L, dual_isLocallyTrivial hL, ?_⟩
3320	  -- Choose, for each point, a trivialising affine open of `L` together with the
3321	  -- trivialisation `eM x : L|_{U x} ≅ 𝒪_{U x}`.
3322	  choose U hxU _hUaff hLt using hL
3323	  -- The dual trivialises on the SAME open `U x`, derived FROM the `L`-trivialisation
3324	  -- `eM x` (the chain of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`), so both legs descend from one datum
3325	  -- — this is what makes the overlap cocycle a `g · g⁻¹ = 1` cancellation.
3326	  set eM : ∀ x, L.restrict (U x).ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
3327	    fun x => (hLt x).some with heM
3328	  set eN : ∀ x, (dual L).restrict (U x).ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
3329	    fun x => dual_restrict_iso (U x).ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso (eM x)).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso with heN
3330	  -- Local contraction iso `(L ⊗ dual L)|_{U x} ≅ 𝒪_{U x}` — the exact chain of
3331	  -- `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`: restrict-commutes-with-⊗, bifunctoriality, unit.
3332	  set e : ∀ x, (tensorObj L (dual L)).restrict (U x).ι ≅
3333	      SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
3334	    fun x => tensorObj_restrict_iso (U x).ι L (dual L) ≪≫
3335	      tensorObjIsoOfIso (eM x) (eN x) ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso with he
3336	  -- Identify the restricted global unit `𝒪_X|_{U x}` with the local unit `𝒪_{U x}`
3337	  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` ≫ `pullbackUnitIso`).
3338	  set uι : ∀ x, restrict (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (U x).ι ≅
3339	      SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=

python3 - "$@" <<'PY'
import sys, re
files="""AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjInverse.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/LineBundlePullback.lean""".split()
import glob
files+=glob.glob("AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/**/*.lean",recursive=True)
files=sorted(set(files))
for f in files:
    src=open(f).read()
    # strip block comments /- ... -/ (nested-ish, simple)
    # remove line comments
    lines=src.split('\n')
    depth=0
    out=[]
    for i,l in enumerate(lines,1):
        j=0; buf=''; k=0
        # crude: track block comment depth char by char
        s=l
        res=''
        idx=0
        while idx<len(s):
            two=s[idx:idx+2]
            if depth==0 and two=='--':
                break
            if two=='/-':
                depth+=1; idx+=2; continue
            if two=='-/' and depth>0:
                depth-=1; idx+=2; continue
            if depth==0:
                res+=s[idx]
            idx+=1
        if depth==0 and 'sorry' in res:
            # ignore backtick-quoted
            # remove backtick spans
            res2=re.sub(r'`[^`]*`','',res)
            if 'sorry' in res2:
                out.append((i,l.strip()))
    if out:
        print("### ",f)
        for i,l in out: print(i,":",l)
PY

data.content:
step is no longer blocked and the iter-218 "infrastructure-missing" gate is retired.
The closure is now assembled WITHOUT the categorical "invertible object ⇒ inverse"
escape (still unavailable — no `MonoidalCategory (X.Modules)` for the varying
structure sheaf, §2) and WITHOUT the forbidden sheafify-the-presheaf-evaluation
shortcut (it re-hits the `M ◁ η` whiskering = the abandoned tensor-stalk "d.2"
gap, a DEAD END — analogist `ts226descent.md`, verdict D). Instead it glues local
trivialising data, touching no tensor stalk. The C-bridge `dual_isLocallyTrivial`,
A-bridge `homOfLocalCompat`, and B-bridge `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` are all
implemented; the remaining blocker is `trivialisation_restrict_compat` (the per-chart
restrict naturality telescope, see body comment). EXACT decomposition:
`informal/exists_tensorObj_inverse.md` and `analogies/ts226descent.md`.
-/
lemma exists_tensorObj_inverse {X : Scheme.{u}} {L : X.Modules}
    (hL : LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial L) :
    ∃ Linv : X.Modules, LineBundle.IsLocallyTrivial Linv ∧
      Nonempty (tensorObj L Linv ≅ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) :=
  by
  classical
  -- `Linv := dual L`; locally trivial by the **C-bridge** `dual_isLocallyTrivial`.
  refine ⟨dual L, dual_isLocallyTrivial hL, ?_⟩
  -- Choose, for each point, a trivialising affine open of `L` together with the
  -- trivialisation `eM x : L|_{U x} ≅ 𝒪_{U x}`.
  choose U hxU _hUaff hLt using hL
  -- The dual trivialises on the SAME open `U x`, derived FROM the `L`-trivialisation
  -- `eM x` (the chain of `dual_isLocallyTrivial`), so both legs descend from one datum
  -- — this is what makes the overlap cocycle a `g · g⁻¹ = 1` cancellation.
  set eM : ∀ x, L.restrict (U x).ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
    fun x => (hLt x).some with heM
  set eN : ∀ x, (dual L).restrict (U x).ι ≅ SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
    fun x => dual_restrict_iso (U x).ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso (eM x)).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso with heN
  -- Local contraction iso `(L ⊗ dual L)|_{U x} ≅ 𝒪_{U x}` — the exact chain of
  -- `tensorObj_isLocallyTrivial`: restrict-commutes-with-⊗, bifunctoriality, unit.
  set e : ∀ x, (tensorObj L (dual L)).restrict (U x).ι ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
    fun x => tensorObj_restrict_iso (U x).ι L (dual L) ≪≫
      tensorObjIsoOfIso (eM x) (eN x) ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso with he
  -- Identify the restricted global unit `𝒪_X|_{U x}` with the local unit `𝒪_{U x}`
  -- (`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` ≫ `pullbackUnitIso`).
  set uι : ∀ x, restrict (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (U x).ι ≅
      SheafOfModules.unit (U x : Scheme).ringCatSheaf :=
    fun x => (Scheme.Modules.restrictFunctorIsoPullback (U x).ι).app
        (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) ≪≫ pullbackUnitIso (U x).ι with huι
  -- Local morphisms `f x : (L ⊗ dual L)|_{U x} ⟶ 𝒪_X|_{U x}` (the contraction, landed
  -- in the restricted GLOBAL unit so `homOfLocalCompat` can consume them); each is an iso.
  set f : ∀ x, (tensorObj L (dual L)).restrict (U x).ι ⟶
      restrict (SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf) (U x).ι :=
    fun x => (e x).hom ≫ (uι x).inv with hf_def
  have hfiso : ∀ x, IsIso (f x) := by
    intro x; rw [hf_def]; infer_instance
  -- Glue the `f x` to a single global morphism `ε : L ⊗ dual L ⟶ 𝒪_X` via the
  -- **A-bridge** `homOfLocalCompat`.  Its hypothesis is the sectionwise overlap
  -- agreement (cocycle):  on `V ≤ U i ⊓ U j` the conjugated components of `f i`, `f j`
  -- coincide — the `g_{ij}·g_{ij}⁻¹ = 1` cancellation of the transition units.
  set ε : tensorObj L (dual L) ⟶ SheafOfModules.unit X.ringCatSheaf :=
    homOfLocalCompat U (fun x => ⟨x, hxU x⟩) f (by
      intro i j V hVi hVj
      simp only [hf_def, he, huι, heN]
      -- Reduce BOTH overlap legs to the single-open-`V` form (`trivialisation_restrict_compat`
      -- applied to `i` and `j`), killing the `(U i).ι⁻¹` vs `(U j).ι⁻¹` reindexing.
      erw [trivialisation_restrict_compat hVi (eM i),
         trivialisation_restrict_compat hVj (eM j)]
      -- The two legs now differ only in the trivialisation refined to `V`.
      set eMi := restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVi (eM i) with hMi
      set eMj := restrictIsoUnitOfLE hVj (eM j) with hMj
      -- Transition unit `t : 𝒪_V ≅ 𝒪_V` with `eMi ≪≫ t = eMj`.
      set t : SheafOfModules.unit (V : Scheme).ringCatSheaf ≅
          SheafOfModules.unit (V : Scheme).ringCatSheaf := eMi.symm ≪≫ eMj with ht_def
      have ht : eMi ≪≫ t = eMj := by
        apply Iso.ext
        rw [ht_def]
        simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom]
        -- `≫` in `SheafOfModules` is defeq-but-not-syntactic, so `rw`/`simp` of category
        -- lemmas fail to pattern-match; term-mode `exact` discharges via unification.
        exact Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc eMi eMj.hom
      -- Factor the dual leg of `eMj` as `dualLeg eMi ≪≫ sConj` by inserting `du ≪≫ du.symm = 𝟙`.
      have hfact :
          dual_restrict_iso V.ι L ≪≫
              ((dualIsoOfIso eMi).symm ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso t).symm) ≪≫ dual_unit_iso
            = (dual_restrict_iso V.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eMi).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso) ≪≫
              (dual_unit_iso.symm ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso t).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso) := by
        apply Iso.ext
        simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Iso.symm_hom, Category.assoc]
        rw [Iso.hom_inv_id_assoc]
      -- Core iso equation: the two `tensorObjIsoOfIso ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso` middles agree.
      -- RHS collapses to LHS via `dualIsoOfIso_trans` (order flips) + `tensorObjIsoOfIso_trans`
      -- + `tensorObj_unit_self_duality_collapse t` (the `g·g⁻¹ = 1` cancellation).
      have hiso :
          tensorObjIsoOfIso eMi
              (dual_restrict_iso V.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eMi).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso) ≪≫
            tensorObj_unit_iso
          = tensorObjIsoOfIso eMj
              (dual_restrict_iso V.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eMj).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso) ≪≫
            tensorObj_unit_iso := by
        rw [← ht, dualIsoOfIso_trans, Iso.trans_symm, hfact, tensorObjIsoOfIso_trans,
          Iso.trans_assoc, tensorObj_unit_self_duality_collapse t]
      -- Lift to the shared `tensorObj_restrict_iso ≪≫ … ≪≫ tensorObj_unit_iso` wrapper.
      have hchain :
          tensorObj_restrict_iso V.ι L (dual L) ≪≫
              tensorObjIsoOfIso eMi
                (dual_restrict_iso V.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eMi).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso) ≪≫
              tensorObj_unit_iso
            = tensorObj_restrict_iso V.ι L (dual L) ≪≫
              tensorObjIsoOfIso eMj
                (dual_restrict_iso V.ι L ≪≫ (dualIsoOfIso eMj).symm ≪≫ dual_unit_iso) ≪≫
              tensorObj_unit_iso :=
        congrArg (fun w => tensorObj_restrict_iso V.ι L (dual L) ≪≫ w) hiso
      -- Both legs are now `((wrapper).hom ≫ (uι V).inv).val.app _` conjugated by the SAME
      -- `eqToHom`s; rewriting the wrapper iso makes them syntactically identical.
      rw [hchain]) with hεdef
  -- `ε` is a global iso since it restricts to the iso `f x` on each cover member `U x`
  -- (**B-bridge** `isIso_of_isIso_restrict`).
  have hεiso : IsIso ε := by
    refine isIso_of_isIso_restrict ε U hxU ?_
    intro x
    have key : (restrictFunctor (U x).ι).map ε = f x := by
      rw [hεdef]
      exact homOfLocalCompat_restrictFunctor_map U _ f _ x
    rw [key]; exact hfiso x
  exact ⟨asIso ε⟩

end Modules

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry

data.content: Launching skill: leandag

text:
Base directory for this skill: /AI4M/users/Axel/LeanAlgebraicGeometry-Horizon/.claude/skills/leandag

The DAG links blueprint nodes by `\uses` edges. An edge `source -> target` means
*target depends on source*. The engine records each node's `\lean` link,
`proved`/`leanok` status, effort metrics, and Lean source metadata.

## Keep It Fresh

- Run the project build after Lean/blueprint edits so generated Lean artifacts and
  imported libraries are current.
- Run `horizon blueprint` after blueprint or Lean declaration changes; it writes
  `.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` for the dashboard and cached DAG
  consumers.
- Use `horizon leandag` for live inspection when deciding what to prove next.

## `horizon leandag` (preferred)

- `horizon leandag` — per-project summary: nodes, edges, proved count, and any
  isolated nodes (no edge in or out — usually a blueprint bug to fix).
- `horizon leandag -p <project> --node <label>` — one node's direct deps, full
  transitive cone, and reverse deps.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --union` — merge projects by declaration/node id.
  Same-id nodes are combined; divergent non-signature metadata is tolerated, but
  a Lean signature mismatch is an error to investigate.
- `horizon leandag -p a -p b --intersect` — only shared declaration/node ids,
  with the same signature check.
- Add `--json` for machine-readable output.

A node is "ready" when all dependencies are proved. Prefer ready nodes when
choosing what to attempt next.

## Cached JSON

`horizon blueprint` writes each project's DAG to
`.archon-horizon/blueprints/<project>.json` (`nodes`, `edges`, `dangling`) for
the dashboard. Under the leandag engine, `\uses` are resolved at build/cache
time, so `dangling` is empty there; the parser fallback still populates it. To
check for orphan/unresolved nodes, use `horizon leandag` rather than relying only
on `dangling`.

=== hom_of_local_compat_restrict block (6660-6680) ===




[The glued morphism restricts to its local datum]
  \label{lem:hom_of_local_compat_restrict}
  % NOTE: iter-099 — the v4.31 recovery dropped this decl from DualInverse.lean; it is currently a
  % private re-port in TensorObjInverse.lean. \lean{} name still resolves; pending upstream-restore decision.
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.homOfLocalCompat_restrictFunctor_map}
  \uses{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat, lem:scheme_modules_top_section_to_hom_app}
  In the situation of \cref{lem:sheafofmodules_hom_of_local_compat} --- a cover \(\{U_i\}\)
  of \(X\) and a compatible family \(f_i : M|_{U_i} \to N|_{U_i}\) --- the restriction of the
  glued global morphism back to a cover member recovers its local datum:
  \[
    (\mathtt{restrictFunctor}\,U_i).\mathtt{map}\,(\mathtt{homOfLocalCompat}\,f) \;=\; f_i .
  \]
  This is the companion gluing-value identity needed to apply the B-connector
  \cref{lem:isiso_of_isiso_restrict}: when every \(f_i\) is an isomorphism, this identity
  transports that to the restriction of the glued morphism, so the global morphism is
  locally an isomorphism and hence an isomorphism.
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}



  By construction, \(\mathtt{homOfLocalCompat}\) produces its global morphism by gluing
  the local sections of the hom-sheaf associated to each \(f_i\) along the cover; the sheaf
  gluing axiom states that the resulting global section restricts on \(U_i\) to the section
  corresponding to \(f_i\). Converting the global section back to a morphism is sectionwise
  faithful (\cref{lem:scheme_modules_top_section_to_hom_app}), and the section-to-morphism
  construction preserves the underlying section, so the restriction of the global morphism to
  \(U_i\) is sectionwise equal to \(f_i\) --- hence equal as a morphism.
\end{proof}

=== presheafDualUnitIso_naturality block (10650-10695) ===



[Naturality of the presheaf dual-of-unit iso against a unit automorphism (B1 eval-core)]
  \label{lem:presheafdualunitiso_naturality}
  % NOTE: iter-099 — the v4.31 recovery dropped this decl from DualInverse.lean; it is currently a
  % private re-port in TensorObjInverse.lean. \lean{} name still resolves; pending upstream-restore decision.
  \lean{AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Modules.presheafDualUnitIso_naturality}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:dual_unit_iso_gen, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}
  Let \(\hat s\) be a presheaf-level automorphism of the unit \(\mathbf 1\) (the underlying map of a
  sheaf-level unit automorphism \(s\)). The presheaf dual-of-unit isomorphism
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} : \mathtt{dual}\,\mathbf 1 \cong \mathbf 1\) is natural in
  \(\hat s\) for the contravariant transport on the source:
  \[
    \mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\hat s \;\mathbin{;}\; \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}
      = \mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso} \;\mathbin{;}\; \hat s.
  \]
\end{lemma}
\begin{proof}
  \uses{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso, def:dual_unit_iso_gen, lem:internal_hom_eval, lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}
  By construction (\cref{def:dual_unit_iso_gen}, \cref{def:presheaf_dual_unit_iso})
  \(\mathtt{presheafDualUnitIso}\) is the sectionwise evaluation-at-\(1\) map of the internal hom of
  the unit into itself. On sections, \(\mathtt{dualIsoOfIso}\,\hat s\) is precomposition by the
  underlying map of \(\hat s\) (\cref{lem:presheaf_dualisoofiso_trans}), so the left-hand composite
  evaluates a dual section \(\varphi\) at \(1\) after precomposing by \(\hat s\), i.e.\ it computes
  \(\varphi(\hat s(1))\). Since \(\hat s\) is a unit automorphism --- multiplication by a unit scalar
  --- it commutes with evaluation, so this equals \(\hat s\) applied to \(\varphi(1)\), the
  right-hand composite. Equality of the two evaluation-at-\(1\) maps is the naturality of the
  internal-hom evaluation (\cref{lem:internal_hom_eval}) specialised at the unit.
\end{proof}
